breadcrumbs.title: Hunt Me I Payment Collectors

headings.text: Hunt Me I: Payment Collectors
Scenario
1-What was the name of the ZIP attachment that Michael downloaded?
InvoiceAT2023-227.zip
2-What was the contained file that Michael extracted from the attachment?
PaymentInvoice.pdf.lnk.lnk
3-What was the name of the command-line process that spawned from the extracted file attachment?
powershell.exe
4-What URL did the attacker use to download a tool to establish a reverse shell connection?
https://raw.githubusercontent.com/besimorhino/powercat/master/powercat.ps1
5-What port did the workstation connect to the attacker on?
6-What was the first native Windows binary the attacker ran for system enumeration after obtaining remote access?
systeminfo.exe
7-What is the URL of the script that the attacker downloads to enumerate the domain?
8-What was the name of the file share that the attacker mapped to Michael's workstation?
SSF-FinancialRecords
9-What directory did the attacker copy the contents of the file share to?
C:\Users\michael.ascot\downloads\exfiltration
10-What was the name of the Excel file the attacker extracted from the file share?
ClientPortfolioSummary.xlsx
11-What was the name of the archive file that the attacker created to prepare for exfiltration?
exfilt8me.zip
12-What is the MITRE ID of the technique that the attacker used to exfiltrate the data?
13-What was the domain of the attacker's server that retrieved the exfiltrated data?
haz4rdw4re.io
14-The attacker exfiltrated an additional file from the victim's workstation. What is the flag you receive after reconstructing the file?

overview: On Friday, September 15, 2023, Michael Ascot, a Senior Finance Director from SwiftSpend, was checking his emails in Outlook and came across an email appearing to be from Abotech Waste Management regarding a monthly invoice for their services. Michael actioned this email and downloaded the attachment to his workstation without thinking.

study.overview: On Friday, September 15, 2023, Michael Ascot, a Senior Finance Director from SwiftSpend, was checking his emails in Outlook and came across an email appearing to be from Abotech Waste Management regarding a monthly invoice for their services. Michael actioned this email and downloaded the attachment to his workstation without thinking.
study.whySoc: Use this note as an analyst reference: understand the concept, identify the relevant telemetry, pivot through evidence, and document the decision path clearly.
keyConcepts: 1-What was the name of the ZIP attachment that Michael downloaded?
2-What was the contained file that Michael extracted from the attachment?
3-What was the name of the command-line process that spawned from the extracted file attachment?
4-What URL did the attacker use to download a tool to establish a reverse shell connection?
5-What port did the workstation connect to the attacker on?
workflow: Identify the user, host, process, network flow, or identity object involved.
Collect the highest-value logs first and preserve timestamps and assumptions.
Pivot through related notes, telemetry sources, and MITRE references.
Document findings, gaps, containment options, and detection improvements.
tips: nslookup.exe is a built-in Windows tool for DNS lookups, but attackers can misuse it for data exfiltration.
